nodes.label: Fully autonomous research pipeline
nodes.description: Accept a topic, retrieve sources, synthesize findings, and emit a report without human intervention.

nodes.label: Well-formatted cited reports
nodes.description: Final output must be human-readable with clear sections, citations, and references.

nodes.label: Support ~200 concurrent queries/day
nodes.description: System must handle parallel research queries on a single local machine without queue starvation or resource exhaustion.

nodes.label: Minimal human input post-start
nodes.description: Once a query is submitted, the pipeline runs to completion or structured failure with no prompts.

nodes.label: All claims must carry source citations
nodes.description: Every extracted fact or summary block in the report must link back to its source URL and retrieval timestamp.

nodes.label: Graceful degradation on partial failures
nodes.description: API errors, missing sources, or scraping failures must not abort the full pipeline — partial results are valid output.

nodes.label: Deduplicate and reconcile conflicting sources
nodes.description: Duplicate content across sources must be collapsed; conflicting claims must be flagged, not silently merged.

nodes.label: Test suite must not require live API calls
nodes.description: All tests must be runnable offline using fixtures, mocks, and recorded responses.

nodes.label: Use Tavily as the search and retrieval API
nodes.description: Tavily selected as the primary web search provider, returning full cleaned page content per result.
nodes.rationale: Purpose-built for AI agent pipelines — returns full page text, includes relevance scoring, eliminates a separate scraping layer. Python SDK integrates cleanly with asyncio.
alternatives_considered: Serper (snippets only, requires scraping layer)
Bing Search API (snippet-based, higher per-call cost)
Google Custom Search API (quota limits, snippet-only on free tier)
SerpAPI (costly at scale)

nodes.label: Use Claude (Anthropic API) for summarization and synthesis
nodes.description: Claude is the LLM backbone for per-source summarization, conflict detection, and final synthesis.
nodes.rationale: Large context window handles multi-source synthesis in a single pass. Structured output via tool use enables reliable JSON for citation tracking. Strong instruction-following for report formatting.
alternatives_considered: OpenAI GPT-4o (comparable capability, different pricing)
Ollama local models (no API cost, slower on CPU, weaker synthesis quality)
Gemini 1.5 Pro (large context, less mature tooling ecosystem)

nodes.label: Custom async Python pipeline (asyncio)
nodes.description: The pipeline is a hand-rolled async DAG in Python rather than a framework like LangGraph or Prefect.
nodes.rationale: Fixed, well-defined stage sequence. Custom DAG gives full control over error boundaries, retry logic, and semaphore-based concurrency without framework overhead.
alternatives_considered: LangChain/LangGraph (abstraction overhead, harder to debug)
Prefect/Dagster (suited for ETL, not interactive agent pipelines)
Celery + Redis (requires Redis server, adds infra complexity on local machine)

nodes.label: SQLite for job state, filesystem for artifacts
nodes.description: SQLite tracks job metadata, source hashes for dedup, dead-letter entries, and cost records. Raw content and final reports live as files on disk.
nodes.rationale: No server to manage. SQLite is sufficient for 200 jobs/day on a single machine. File-per-artifact is simple and inspectable.
alternatives_considered: PostgreSQL (server overhead, unnecessary for local)
DuckDB (good for analytics, less suited for transactional job state)
Pure filesystem JSON sidecars (loses queryability for dedup lookups)

nodes.label: asyncio with bounded semaphore and per-API token buckets
nodes.description: Concurrent queries managed via asyncio semaphore (pipeline-level cap) plus per-API token bucket rate limiters for Tavily and Claude independently.
nodes.rationale: 200/day with burst concurrency. Semaphore cap prevents memory exhaustion. Separate token buckets per API prevent one API's rate limit from starving the other.
alternatives_considered: multiprocessing (CPU-bound; this pipeline is I/O-bound)
ThreadPoolExecutor (GIL limits parallelism)
Ray (overkill for single-machine target)

nodes.label: Markdown primary output, PDF via WeasyPrint
nodes.description: Markdown is the canonical report artifact; PDF is derived via WeasyPrint.
nodes.rationale: Markdown is version-controllable and diff-able. WeasyPrint converts HTML/CSS to PDF without requiring LaTeX or headless Chrome.
alternatives_considered: LaTeX (high-quality PDF but heavy dependency)
ReportLab (verbose, poor for narrative text)
Pandoc (excellent but adds a system binary dependency)

nodes.label: Exponential backoff × 4, then dead-letter queue + structured log alert
nodes.description: All external API calls retry with 1s/2s/4s/8s backoff. On 4th failure the job enters dead_letter_queue in SQLite and a structured log entry is written at ERROR level.
nodes.rationale: Transient errors (429, 503) are common in API-heavy pipelines. Four attempts covers most transient spikes. Dead-letter queue ensures no silent data loss. Structured log is sufficient for local-machine developer response.
alternatives_considered: Fail fast with no retry (too fragile)
Infinite retry (risks live-lock under sustained outage)
Push notification channel (over-engineered for local setup; extensible later)

nodes.label: Fluent narrative synthesis with post-hoc citation verification
nodes.description: Claude generates a flowing narrative report, then a second LLM pass checks each paragraph for citation coverage and flags or injects missing references.
nodes.rationale: Fluent narrative is more readable for human consumers. Post-hoc verification catches most attribution gaps without forcing a mechanical citation-per-sentence constraint during generation.
alternatives_considered: Strictly grounded synthesis (more reliable but mechanical prose)
No verification pass (faster but risks unchecked hallucination drift)

nodes.label: Shallow deduplication via URL normalisation + SHA-256 content hash
nodes.description: Sources deduplicated by normalised URL and SHA-256 hash of full text. Matches on either key collapse to the first-seen copy.
nodes.rationale: Fast, zero extra API cost, no additional model dependency. Catches syndicated articles, mirror sites, and cached pages.
alternatives_considered: Semantic embedding similarity (catches paraphrased duplicates but adds model dependency and latency)
Title similarity only (too coarse)

nodes.label: Conflicting claims get a side-by-side callout box in the report
nodes.description: When the Conflict Detector flags a contradiction, the Synthesis Engine renders a visually distinct callout block presenting both claims with citations, letting the reader judge.
nodes.rationale: Presenting both claims preserves epistemic honesty. A callout box makes conflicts visually salient rather than burying them in prose.
alternatives_considered: Favour higher-relevance source and footnote dissent (suppresses information)
Merge claims silently (misleading and unreliable)

nodes.label: Per-query token budget with relevance-ordered truncation and cost alerts
nodes.description: Each query has a configurable max token budget. Sources sorted by Tavily relevance score; lower-relevance sources truncated/skipped if budget exceeded. Daily cost tracked in SQLite with log WARNING at threshold.
nodes.rationale: Prevents unbounded cost growth at 200 queries/day. Relevance-ordered truncation maximises information quality per token spent. Soft alert without hard kill allows operator tuning.
alternatives_considered: Cost tracking only without hard limits (risk of runaway spend)
Uniform truncation of all sources equally (wastes budget on low-relevance content)
No token management (not viable for 200/day)

nodes.label: Structured log file as sole developer notification channel
nodes.description: Dead-letter events, cost threshold alerts, and pipeline errors written as structured JSON log entries via structlog to a rotating log file.
nodes.rationale: Sufficient for a local-machine setup. Structlog JSON output is machine-parseable for future integration with log shippers or alerting tools.
alternatives_considered: Email via SMTP (extensible future option)
Slack/Discord webhook (extensible future option)
Plain print/stderr (not machine-parseable)

nodes.label: YAML config file with environment variable overrides
nodes.description: All pipeline parameters live in config.yaml. Environment variables override individual keys for CI and secrets management.
nodes.rationale: YAML is human-readable and version-controllable. Env var override is the standard pattern for CI execution without modifying config files.
alternatives_considered: Env vars only (no single source of truth)
Hardcoded defaults (not operator-tunable)
Pydantic Settings only (requires Python knowledge to configure)

nodes.label: Proceed with report but embed quality_flags warning below 3 sources
nodes.description: If fewer than 3 usable sources remain after dedup and paywall filtering, the pipeline proceeds to synthesis but marks the report as low-confidence with a prominent header flag describing the source coverage limitation.
nodes.rationale: Aborting the job loses all partial value; a flagged report lets the human reader make an informed judgment about trustworthiness. Three sources is the minimum for meaningful triangulation.
alternatives_considered: Abort job and return structured error (loses all partial work)
Proceed silently without flagging (misleads reader about coverage)

nodes.label: Configurable TTL: 7-day purge for intermediates, longer retention for final reports
nodes.description: A nightly cleanup job deletes raw Tavily content and source chunk files older than TTL_INTERMEDIATE_DAYS (default 7). Final Markdown/PDF reports are retained for TTL_REPORT_DAYS (default 90). SQLite metadata is kept indefinitely.
nodes.rationale: Intermediate artifacts are large and only needed for debugging active jobs. Final reports are the primary deliverable and should persist. SQLite records are small and valuable for run history and dedup lookups.
alternatives_considered: Keep everything indefinitely (disk exhaustion at 200/day)
Delete all intermediates immediately on completion (loses debuggability for failed jobs)
No TTL policy (undocumented and inconsistent)

nodes.label: CLI supports both single-query and batch file modes
nodes.description: python -m researcher "<topic>" for single-query. python -m researcher --batch queries.txt for batch mode. Batch mode submits all queries concurrently up to the semaphore limit.
nodes.rationale: Single-query mode enables quick testing and one-off research tasks. Batch mode is the primary interface for the 200-queries/day scalability target.
alternatives_considered: Single-query only (cannot drive 200/day workload efficiently)
Batch only (poor developer experience for testing and one-off use)

nodes.label: Job Manager
nodes.description: Accepts research query input via CLI (single or batch), creates job records in SQLite, acquires semaphore slots, and dispatches the pipeline DAG. Tracks job lifecycle: queued → running → complete → failed.

nodes.label: Search & Retrieval Engine
nodes.description: Calls Tavily API with the research topic, retrieves N results with full page content, and returns a list of raw SearchResult objects. Wrapped by Retry Manager and Rate Limiter.

nodes.label: Content Processor
nodes.description: Deduplicates results by URL normalisation and SHA-256 hash, strips boilerplate, chunks content into token-bounded segments, applies relevance-ordered token budget, and enforces the 3-source minimum threshold.

nodes.label: Per-Source Summarizer
nodes.description: Calls Claude once per deduplicated source chunk to produce a structured SourceSummary with inline attribution tags. Runs concurrently across sources within the token budget.

nodes.label: Conflict Detector
nodes.description: Scans the set of SourceSummary objects for contradictory claims. Emits ConflictFlag records with both source references, confidence score, and the verbatim conflicting claims.

nodes.label: Synthesis Engine
nodes.description: Calls Claude with all source summaries, conflict flags, quality flags, and a structured report template. Renders a fluent narrative with conflict callout boxes wherever ConflictFlags exist.

nodes.label: Citation Verifier
nodes.description: Runs a second LLM pass over the synthesized report. Checks citation presence and groundedness per paragraph against source summaries. Flags or auto-injects missing references.

nodes.label: Report Renderer
nodes.description: Transforms the verified ReportSchema into structured Markdown with a references section, then renders a PDF via WeasyPrint using a CSS template.

nodes.label: Retry Manager
nodes.description: Decorator/wrapper applied to all external API calls. Implements exponential backoff (1s/2s/4s/8s × 4 attempts). On exhaustion, writes to dead_letter_queue in SQLite and triggers the Notifier.

nodes.label: Rate Limiter
nodes.description: Maintains a global asyncio semaphore for pipeline-level concurrency and independent per-API token buckets for Tavily and Claude.

nodes.label: Token Budget Manager
nodes.description: Enforces per-query token budgets by ordering sources by relevance score and truncating or skipping lower-relevance sources when budget would be exceeded. Tracks per-call token usage.

nodes.label: Cost Tracker
nodes.description: Accumulates token usage and estimated API cost per job in SQLite. Compares running daily total against configurable alert threshold and logs a structured WARNING when exceeded.

nodes.label: Job Store (SQLite via SQLAlchemy async)
nodes.description: Persists job records, source content hashes, dead-letter entries, cost records, and run history. Accessed via SQLAlchemy async session.

nodes.label: Developer Notifier
nodes.description: Writes structured JSON log entries via structlog for dead-letter events, cost threshold breaches, and pipeline errors. Outputs to a rotating log file. Designed for future extension to email/webhook.

nodes.label: Config Manager
nodes.description: Loads config.yaml at startup, merges environment variable overrides, and exposes a typed Settings object (via Pydantic) consumed by all components.

nodes.label: Artifact Cleanup Job
nodes.description: Nightly scheduled coroutine that deletes raw Tavily content and source chunk files older than TTL_INTERMEDIATE_DAYS (default 7). Final reports deleted after TTL_REPORT_DAYS (default 90). SQLite metadata retained indefinitely.

nodes.label: CLI Entry Point
nodes.description: Typer-based CLI providing two modes: single-query (researcher "<topic>") and batch file (researcher --batch queries.txt). Batch mode reads one query per line and submits all to Job Manager concurrently up to the semaphore cap.

nodes.label: SearchResult schema
nodes.description: Normalised output contract from the Search Engine.
nodes.contract: { url: str, title: str, raw_content: str, relevance_score: float, retrieved_at: datetime }

nodes.label: SourceChunk schema
nodes.description: Token-bounded content unit output by the Content Processor, ready for LLM ingestion.
nodes.contract: { chunk_id: str, url: str, content_hash: str, text: str, token_count: int, chunk_index: int, relevance_score: float }

nodes.label: SourceSummary schema
nodes.description: Structured per-source summary with attribution, output by the Summarizer.
nodes.contract: { summary_id: str, url: str, title: str, key_points: list[str], conflicts: list[str], token_cost: int, retrieved_at: datetime }

nodes.label: ConflictFlag schema
nodes.description: Conflict record emitted by Conflict Detector and consumed by the Synthesis Engine.
nodes.contract: { conflict_id: str, claim_a: str, source_a_url: str, claim_b: str, source_b_url: str, confidence: float, subject: str }

nodes.label: Final Report schema
nodes.description: Structured report contract passed from Synthesis Engine through Citation Verifier to Report Renderer.
nodes.contract: { title: str, executive_summary: str, quality_flags: list[str], sections: list[{heading, body, citations, conflict_callouts}], references: list[{id, url, title, retrieved_at}] }

nodes.label: Content Processor dedup and chunking unit tests
nodes.description: Loads fixture HTML and raw text inputs. Asserts correct URL normalisation, SHA-256 deduplication, boilerplate stripping, token-bounded chunk output, and quality_flag injection when source count < 3.

nodes.label: Retry Manager backoff and DLQ unit tests
nodes.description: Injects callables that fail N times then succeed (or always fail). Asserts correct backoff timing, attempt count, dead-letter queue write, and notifier trigger on exhaustion.

nodes.label: Interface schema conformance tests
nodes.description: Validates recorded Tavily and Claude API responses against Pydantic models for all four interface schemas. Ensures schema contracts hold against real API response shapes.

nodes.label: Summarizer unit tests with frozen LLM fixtures
nodes.description: Feeds frozen Claude response fixtures into the Summarizer. Asserts correct SourceSummary shape, attribution presence, and token cost recording.

nodes.label: Citation Verifier unit tests
nodes.description: Provides a ReportSchema with known uncited paragraphs and frozen LLM verification responses. Asserts correct flag injection and reference resolution.

nodes.label: Full pipeline integration test (all externals mocked)
nodes.description: Runs the complete pipeline from CLI entry through Job Manager to Report Renderer with all Tavily and Claude calls replaced by recorded fixture responses. Asserts final report structure, citation presence, conflict callout rendering, and quality_flags.

nodes.label: Token Budget Manager enforcement unit tests
nodes.description: Injects a set of sources exceeding the budget limit. Asserts that lower-relevance sources are truncated first, budget is never exceeded, and cost tracker receives correct token counts.

nodes.label: Report Renderer output tests
nodes.description: Feeds a fixed ReportSchema with known sections, citations, and conflict callouts. Asserts Markdown structure (headings, callout blocks, references section) and that PDF generation completes without error.

nodes.label: Artifact cleanup TTL unit tests
nodes.description: Creates fixture artifact files with manipulated mtimes. Asserts that files older than TTL_INTERMEDIATE_DAYS are deleted and final reports within TTL_REPORT_DAYS are retained.

nodes.label: Tavily API rate limits accommodate burst concurrency
nodes.description: Assumes Tavily's plan tier allows enough requests/minute for 10 concurrent pipelines each making 3–5 search calls without throttling.

nodes.label: Multi-source summaries fit Claude's context window for synthesis
nodes.description: Assumes combined SourceSummary objects for a single topic fit within Claude's context window after per-source summarization reduces raw content volume.

nodes.label: Local machine sustains 5–10 concurrent async pipelines
nodes.description: Assumes enough RAM and network bandwidth for concurrent Tavily fetches and LLM calls without thrashing.

nodes.label: Tavily returns substantive full-page content reliably
nodes.description: Assumes Tavily successfully extracts readable text from the majority of retrieved URLs vs. returning empty or JS-only shells.

nodes.label: Post-hoc citation pass catches most attribution gaps
nodes.description: Assumes a second LLM verification pass is sufficient to catch uncited claims in the fluent narrative.

nodes.label: Structured log file is sufficient for developer incident response
nodes.description: Assumes the developer monitors log output during pipeline operation and can respond to dead-letter or cost-alert entries without push notifications.

nodes.label: LLM + search API costs escalate at scale
nodes.description: At 200 queries/day with multiple LLM calls per pipeline stage, token costs could become significant without a cost ceiling.

nodes.label: API rate limits throttle or fail concurrent pipelines
nodes.description: Bursts of concurrent queries may exhaust Tavily or Claude API rate limits, causing cascading failures across multiple pipelines.

nodes.label: LLM hallucination survives post-hoc citation check
nodes.description: Fluent narrative generation may introduce plausible-sounding but unsourced claims that the citation verifier fails to catch.

nodes.label: Poor or paywalled source content degrades report quality
nodes.description: Many high-quality sources are paywalled or return minimal content, reducing synthesis quality silently.

nodes.label: Semantic duplicates pass hash-based deduplication
nodes.description: Two sources reporting the same fact in different words pass URL/hash dedup and are treated as independent corroboration.

nodes.label: Dead-letter queue grows unbounded without manual replay
nodes.description: A systemic API outage may accumulate hundreds of DLQ entries with no automated replay mechanism.

nodes.label: Too few usable sources yield a low-confidence report
nodes.description: If paywalls or Tavily extraction failures reduce usable sources below 3, the synthesized report may be thin but not visibly flagged as low-confidence.
